title: Nikkos Tyris

'''Nikkos Tyris''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Anzat (species)|Anzati]] who had served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] during the final decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Tyris fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and became one of the founders of the [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]]. He was one of [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku's]] allies during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and was [[Duel on Susevfi|killed]] by [[Nejaa Halcyon]] during the conflict.

==Biography==
As a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], Tyris and two other Knights fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] after reading ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] documents and becoming disenchanted with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the beginning of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. They believed themselves to be following the ''[[Saarai]]'', which meant "True Way" in ancient [[Sith (language)|Sith]]. Taking the title of [[Saarai-kaar]], Tyris led his "[[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]]," or "hidden followers of truth" in Sith, and the cult grew strong enough to become a concern for the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]. Tyris was approached by [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], leader of the Separatists and the alter ego of a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] known as Darth Tyranus. Dooku gifted Tyris with a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] favored by former Anzati Jedi [[Volfe Karkko]] in exchange for his allegiance.<ref name="JVS" /><ref name="I, Jensaarai">{{PolyhedronCite|157|I, Jensaarai}}</ref><ref name="SWRPG Core">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]''</ref><ref name="Fact File 116">{{FactFile|116|DOO 5-8|Count Dooku}}</ref>

As the war between the Republic and the Separatists continued to ravage, the Jedi Council dispatched the Knights [[Nejaa Halcyon]], [[Ylenic It'kla]], and ''[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Desertwind]]'' to remove the Jensaarai leadership. Attacking them on [[Susevfi]], the Jedi engaged Tyris, Dustrose, and [[Sukarr|another loyal follower]], in an attempt to remove the Jensaari from the war. Recalled as '''Nightsweat''' by Jedi It'kla, the Jedi attempted to turn the Dark Jedi from their path, but were rebuffed. As the [[Duel on Susevfi|battle]] raged on, the other members of the Saarai-kaar fell to the Jedi leaving Tyris alone with Jedi Halcyon. Taking advantage of Halcyon's lack of [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetic]] powers, Tyris was able to [[Shiak|impale]] the [[Human/Legends|Human]] on his [[Color/Legends|azure]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. As Halcyon lay dying, he [[Tutaminis|drained]] Tyris' weapon of its power, and conjured a "fist" of [[The Force/Legends|energy]]. Releasing the energy, Halcyon crushed every bone in the Anzati's body before [[Death/Legends|passing into the Force]]. The release of dark side energies at the deaths of the three Jensaarai leaders collapsed the structure where the rest of the Jensaarai were dwelling, killing all but four of the apprentices. The survivors included the [[Unidentified female Saarai-kaar|wife]] of one of Tyris' apprentices, the woman who would later become the Saarai-kaar of the Jensaarai.<ref name="I, Jensaarai" /><ref name="I, Jedi" />

==Powers and abilities==
Tyris favored the [[Form V/Legends|Shien]] variant of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]], holding his azure blade pointed toward the ground by the strong side hand, with the off side hand riding loosely on the weapon's pommel. He moved the blade in a triangular pattern that effectively warded the lower two-thirds of his body from attack. With this unique style, he could deliver lightning-fast slashes and ripostes that could easily split an opponent from groin to neck, while also rapidly rotating upwards to block overhead strikes.<ref name="I, Jedi" />
